metadata.title: UnionOps | Free Tools & Officer Hub for Union Locals
metadata.description: Free, privacy-first tools for union stewards and officers. Comms stay on your device; if you host an Officer Hub, you control that instance.

home.title: UnionOps: The Toolkit for Local Unions
home.subtitle: Comms tools for volunteers. Officer workflows for presidents and stewards. Built for any local, any union.
home.socialMediaPlanDesc: A quick roadmap: logo, union boards, socials, and a simple website, in that order.
home.pathCommsTitle: New to running a local’s communications?
home.pathCommsDesc: A short roadmap: brand, boards, socials, then a simple website.
home.pathCommsHint: Need copy later? Social Examples and Captions are under Learn.
home.pathOfficerDesc: Track grievances, college bumping, overdue work, and handoffs in the Officer Hub.
home.privacyNote: Comms tools run in your browser. Host an Officer Hub and you control that instance.
home.trustBanner: Local-first Comms: graphics stay in your browser. Host the Officer Hub yourself and you are the data controller for that instance.

social.title: Social Media
social.description: Guides, captions, and graphics for Facebook, Instagram, and more.

print.title: Print
print.description: Flyers and printable materials for rallies, meetings, and events.

boards.title: Union Boards
boards.description: Bare-minimum layouts, print checklists, board notices, and solidarity posters for the workplace.

website.title: Website
website.description: A simple local website template you can host on GitHub Pages.

socialMediaPlan.title: Social Media Plan
socialMediaPlan.subtitle: A quick roadmap for locals starting or improving their communications
socialMediaPlan.intro: Follow these four steps in order. Each builds on the last - brand first, then reach members where they are: on the board, on social, and online.

logo.title: 1. Make a logo
logo.description: Set up your Brand Kit with local number, colours, and logo. Consistent branding makes every post and notice look professional.
checklist: Complete onboarding or open Brand Kit

boards.title: 2. Union boards
boards.description: Post a high-contrast notice on workplace bulletin boards. Members who are not on social still need to see meeting times and updates.
checklist: Choose a notice type and fill in date, time, and location
Post in approved union board locations

socials.title: 3. Socials
socials.description: Share the same message on Facebook and Instagram. Use the Blueprint for tone and frequency, and pre-written captions to save time.
checklist: Read The Blueprint for platform and posting tips
Create a graphic with Graphic Maker or Resizer
Copy a caption from the library and add alt-text

website.title: 4. A website
website.description: Give members one link for contact info, officers, and about your local. Download a ready-made template and host it free on GitHub Pages.
checklist: Fill in about text, officers, and contact email
Preview and download the site ZIP
Follow the deploy guide to publish

guide.title: The Blueprint
guide.subtitle: A step-by-step handbook for starting and running your local's social media.
guide.socialMediaPlanLink: New to comms? Start with the Social Media Plan →

platforms.title: Which platforms to choose
platforms.content: Facebook reaches a broad member demographic and supports longer posts and event pages. Instagram works well for visual storytelling and younger members. Start with one platform, do it well, then expand. Many support-staff and workplace locals find a closed Facebook group is the primary member channel.

tone.title: Tone of voice
tone.content: Be professional but human. Speak as a collective ('we', 'our members') not as management. Celebrate wins, explain setbacks honestly, and always centre member voices. Avoid jargon - write for members reading on their phone during a break.

frequency.title: Posting frequency
frequency.content: Aim for 2–3 posts per week minimum during normal times. Increase during bargaining or strike action. Consistency beats volume - a steady rhythm builds trust. Use a simple content mix: 40% updates, 30% member stories, 20% education, 10% solidarity/culture.

trolls.title: Dealing with trolls and management
trolls.content: Do not engage with bad-faith comments. Hide or delete spam, harassment, and misinformation. For management pushback, stick to facts, cite the collective agreement, and escalate to your communications chair or national rep. Never post confidential bargaining details.

accessibility.title: Accessibility - alt-text
accessibility.content: Every image post needs alt-text describing what's in the image for visually impaired members. Use the Alt-Text Assistant to draft that description, then paste it into the platform’s alt-text field (not the caption). Keep descriptions concise but complete: who, what, where, and any text shown in the graphic.

unionBoardsGuide.title: Union Boards Guide
unionBoardsGuide.subtitle: Workplace bulletin boards reach members who are not on social media - start with a bare-minimum layout, then fill the rest with notices and solidarity posters

bareMinimum.title: Bare-minimum board
bareMinimum.intro: Every local board should answer four questions at a glance: How do I find you online? Who do I call for health and safety? Who is on the Local Executive Committee? What is happening next? Everything else is optional filler.
bareMinimum.tip: Print the always-on pieces once on sturdy stock; refresh only the events / rotating zone. Use solidarity posters to fill empty space so the board never looks abandoned.

header.title: Local header
header.content: Local number, short name or division line, and logo from your Brand Kit. Members should recognize the board from across the room.

socials.title: Socials and digital link
socials.content: QR code or short URL to this hub, your local website, or Facebook group. One clear CTA - “Get support” or “Follow us” - beats a wall of icons.

healthSafety.title: Health and safety
healthSafety.content: JHSC / health & safety contacts, how to report a hazard, and where to find the latest safety bulletin. Keep phone numbers large and current.

lec.title: LEC and steward list
lec.content: Local Executive Committee roles with names and work contacts (or steward office hours). Update after elections - outdated lists erode trust.

events.title: Events or rotating spot
events.content: Membership meetings, bargaining updates, workshops, or a solidarity poster. This is the only zone that should change weekly.

whatToPrint.title: What to print
whatToPrint.intro: Think in layers: permanent identity, standing resources, and a rotating slot.

always.label: Always on the board
always.content: Header/logo, socials QR or URL, health & safety contacts, LEC / steward list.

rotate.label: Rotate often
rotate.content: Meeting notices, bargaining updates, event flyers, and high-energy solidarity posters for empty space.

optional.label: Optional extras
optional.content: Member spotlight (with consent), contract highlight one-pagers, how-to-file-a-grievance tear-offs.

sizes.label: Sizes that work
sizes.content: Letter (8.5×11) for dense contact lists; tabloid (11×17) for notices and posters readable from across the room.

layouts.title: Reference layouts from real locals
layouts.intro: Patterns adapted from workplace boards locals actually run - generic zones, no branded campaign art. Pick one that fits your board shape and stick to it so members learn where to look.
layouts.photosIntro: Photos from active campus boards - use them to see how LEC lists, JHSC, ministry posters, and QR codes sit together in the wild.
layouts.fourQuadrantDesc: Header across the top, socials and health & safety side by side, full-width LEC strip, events along the bottom. The most common break-room layout.
layouts.fourQuadrantBest: Best for: standard cork boards in lunch rooms
layouts.priorityStripDesc: Narrow left column keeps socials, H&S, and LEC always visible; the large right panel is reserved for the current campaign, meeting, or solidarity poster.
layouts.priorityStripBest: Best for: wide boards near time clocks
layouts.corridorTallDesc: Single-column stack for narrow hallway boards: header, socials, H&S, LEC, events, then solidarity filler at the bottom so the board never ends in blank cork.
layouts.corridorTallBest: Best for: narrow corridor or door-side boards
layouts.labeledColumnsDesc: Printed section headers across the top - Postings | Updates / LEC | Health & safety + QR | Rights posters. Matches a well-kept campus info board with ministry ESA, Form 82, and bullying/harassment posters on the right.
layouts.labeledColumnsBest: Best for: wide boards you can keep tidy with paper labels
layouts.stewardDeskDesc: Large LEC / steward contact block beside compact socials and H&S cards; bottom strip for the next meeting or action. Common in steward offices where members stop to ask questions.
layouts.stewardDeskBest: Best for: steward office or union room boards

materials.title: Feed the board - posters and templates
materials.intro: When a board looks empty, start with official Ontario posters and a short contact list. Locals do not need to invent content from scratch - print the ministry pieces, then layer local notices and solidarity posters.
materials.ministryNote: PDFs below are for printing. Prefer the official ministry page when checking for newer versions.
materials.templatesNote: Trimmed demos with fake names and campuses - replace with your real LEC, JHSC, and board locations.

items.esaPosterDesc: ESA rights overview - hours, leaves, vacation, termination. Strong default for the health & safety / rights zone.
items.esaPosterAltDesc: Additional ESA poster file for boards that already use an older print run - verify against the current ministry version.
items.form82: In case of injury at work (Form 82)
items.form82Desc: WSIB-style injury reporting poster commonly required beside first-aid information.
items.ohsaDesc: Live statute - link via QR instead of pinning an outdated paper copy of the full Act.
items.ohsaGuideDesc: Plain-language worker rights: know, participate, refuse unsafe work.
items.requiredPostersHubDesc: Ontario hub listing what employers must distribute or post - use this when you are unsure what belongs on the board.
items.boardChecklistDesc: Bare-minimum print list plus QR targets for ESA, OHSA, human rights, and local documents.
items.boardTrackerDesc: Sample multi-campus tracker with fake monitors - track which boards are verified and who checks them.
items.jhscSampleDesc: Short anonymized health & safety committee roster for the H&S zone.
items.ohsaQrTipDesc: One-page tip for members scanning a QR to the live Act from their phone.
items.boardL33Desc: Labeled columns: Postings, updates/LEC list, JHSC roster, ESA + Health & Safety posters, Form 82, bullying/harassment poster, and a large QR. The tidy “bare minimum plus rights” pattern.
items.boardL33Alt: Photograph of a sectioned union info board with labeled columns for postings, LEC, health and safety, and rights posters
items.boardW010Desc: Parent-union banners across the top, Form 82 and bullying/harassment at eye level, LEC list, Health & Safety poster, QR link, plus a small member notes corner - denser but still scannable.
items.boardW010Alt: Photograph of a branded union bulletin board with safety posters, LEC list, QR code, and member notes
items.denseBoardPhotoDesc: High-energy campaign anchors with QR resource sheet and contact cards - use when you need solidarity filler around the standing zones.
items.denseBoardPhotoAlt: Photograph of a densely posted union bulletin board with campaign posters, QR codes, and contact cards

where.title: Where to post
where.content: Use union-designated bulletin boards in break rooms, steward offices, and other agreed locations. Never post on management-controlled boards without permission. Check your collective agreement for posting rights.

howLong.title: How long to leave notices up
howLong.content: Remove outdated notices promptly - a cluttered board loses impact. Meeting notices: post 1–2 weeks ahead, remove after the event. Bargaining updates: refresh weekly during active rounds. Strike notices: update daily during action. Keep the bare-minimum zones current even when the events slot is empty.

whatNot.title: What not to post
whatNot.content: Never post confidential bargaining positions, member home addresses, or internal strategy. Do not name individuals in grievances. If in doubt, ask your local president before printing.

pairing.title: Pair with social media
pairing.content: Use the same headline on your board notice and social graphic so members recognize the message. Post the board notice first for workplace visibility, then share on social with a photo of the posted notice (with consent from anyone visible).

printGuide.title: Print Communications Guide
printGuide.subtitle: When to print and how to pair print with digital channels

when.title: When to print
when.content: Print when members may not see social posts: shift workers, members without smartphones, and high-traffic workplace areas. Flyers work for rallies and picket lines; board notices work for ongoing visibility.

flyers.title: Flyers and handouts
flyers.content: Use the Flyer Maker for picket lines and rallies. Export PDF for letter-size printing at home or a copy shop. Keep text large and high-contrast for outdoor visibility.

boards.title: Board notices
boards.content: Use the Board Notice Maker for workplace bulletin boards. Tabloid (11×17) size is easier to read from a distance. Letter size fits standard union boards.

digital.title: Print plus digital
digital.content: Share the same date, time, and location across board notice, flyer, social graphic, and website. One message, four channels - less confusion for members.

websiteGuide.title: Local Website Guide
websiteGuide.subtitle: Why your local needs a simple website and how to publish one for free

why.title: Why a local website
why.content: Social algorithms change; a website gives members a stable link for contact info, officers, and updates. Employers and media often search for your local online - a professional page builds credibility.

pages.title: What to include
pages.content: Start simple: about your local, executive officers, and contact info. Add bargaining updates during active rounds. Link to your Facebook group and your union's member resources.

deploy.title: GitHub Pages in 5 steps
deploy.content: 1. Create a free GitHub account. 2. Create a new repository (e.g. local243.github.io). 3. Upload the ZIP from the Website Template tool. 4. Go to Settings → Pages → deploy from main branch. 5. Your site will be live at username.github.io within a few minutes.

domain.title: Custom domain (optional)
domain.content: You can point a custom domain (e.g. local243.org) to GitHub Pages. Add a CNAME file with your domain and configure DNS with your registrar. GitHub provides step-by-step instructions in repository Settings.

examples.title: Social Examples
examples.subtitle: Browse strong local post patterns, see why they work, then make your own with the tools.

picket-solidarity.title: Picket line solidarity post
picket-solidarity.description: High-contrast graphic with date, time, and location - a clear call to show up.

why.0: Lead with when and where so members can act without reading a long caption.
why.1: High contrast and a single CTA keep the post readable in a busy feed.

mockup.headline: Picket line tomorrow
mockup.body: Stand with your coworkers. Bring water, signs, and solidarity.

strike-vote-results.title: Strike vote results
strike-vote-results.description: Bold numbers, clear outcome, and the next step for members.

why.0: One big number communicates the mandate faster than a paragraph.
why.1: Pair the result with a short next-step line so members know what follows.

mockup.body: Members voted yes. Stay ready - updates coming.

picket-flyer.title: Picket / rally flyer
picket-flyer.description: Print-ready handout energy adapted for a social share - location first.

why.0: Flyer layouts force hierarchy: event name, place, time.
why.1: Same asset can print for the board and post online.

mockup.headline: Rally & picket
mockup.body: Join us at the main gate. Signs provided.

member-spotlight.title: Member spotlight - years of service
member-spotlight.description: Warm quote, name treatment, and local branding on an Instagram square.

why.0: Faces and short quotes outperform generic announcements for engagement.
why.1: Square crop matches Instagram; keep logo and local ID readable.

mockup.headline: Member spotlight
mockup.body: Twenty years of showing up for our members.

agm-notice.title: AGM notice with agenda highlights
agm-notice.description: Clean layout with date, link or venue, and why attendance matters.

why.0: Date and access info sit above persuasion copy.
why.1: A badge (AGM / Notice) signals urgency without shouting.

mockup.headline: Annual General Meeting
mockup.body: All members welcome - your voice shapes our local.

bargaining-update.title: Bargaining table update
bargaining-update.description: Professional tone: what happened, what we still need, how to stay informed.

why.0: Members want status, not spin - lead with the update.
why.1: Brand colours keep official updates distinct from personal posts.

mockup.headline: Bargaining update
mockup.body: Standing strong for fair wages and safe workplaces.

bargaining-quote.title: Bargaining quote card
bargaining-quote.description: A short leadership quote that carries the room’s resolve into the feed.

why.0: Quotes travel well when they are one idea, not a speech.
why.1: Attribution and local mark build trust that it is an official message.

mockup.headline: Bargaining team
mockup.body: We remain at the table for every member - fair wages, safe workplaces, respect.

town-hall-thanks.title: Town hall thank-you graphic
town-hall-thanks.description: Celebrate turnout with a warm thank-you and one memorable stat.

why.0: Thanking members publicly reinforces participation next time.
why.1: One turnout number is enough - skip the full agenda recap.

mockup.headline: Thank you
mockup.body: 80 members showed up. Together we make the difference.

welcome-members.title: Welcome new members
welcome-members.description: Warm onboarding post that points people to stewards and the local page.

why.0: New members need belonging first, then logistics.
why.1: Invite a next action: talk to a steward or visit the page.

mockup.headline: Welcome to the family
mockup.body: Together we're stronger. Reach out to your steward anytime.

boardNotice.title: Board Notice Maker
boardNotice.subtitle: High-contrast workplace bulletin notices - readable from across the room
boardNotice.headline: Headline

solidarityPoster.title: Solidarity Poster Maker
solidarityPoster.subtitle: High-energy board fillers with big type and solidarity slogans - link members back to this hub for support
solidarityPoster.headline: Headline
solidarityPoster.headlineHint: Use a new line to stack words
solidarityPoster.setupBrandPrompt: Set up your Brand Kit to stamp posters with your local lockup.

qrCard.title: QR Link Card Maker
qrCard.subtitle: Clean attention-grabbing cards for board links - title, optional description, tagline under the QR, and print sizes from full letter down to quarter-sheet
qrCard.savedLinks: Use a saved Brand Kit link
qrCard.sizeTip: Quarter = 4 per letter sheet; Half = 2 per sheet. Print multiple copies of the same card to fill a page.
qrCard.setupBrandPrompt: Set up your Brand Kit to stamp cards with your local lockup.

presets.getSupportDesc: Scan for help, resources, and your local hub.
presets.esaDesc: Employment standards in Ontario - hours, leaves, vacation, and more.
presets.ohsaDesc: Your rights to know, participate, and refuse unsafe work.
presets.healthSafetyDesc: Committee contacts and how to report a hazard.
presets.followUsDesc: Updates, meetings, and solidarity - follow your local online.
presets.localWebsiteDesc: News, contacts, and resources for members.

websiteTemplate.title: Website Template
websiteTemplate.subtitle: Download a simple local website based on the Local 243 model - ready for GitHub Pages
websiteTemplate.referenceNote: This template is based on the live site at local243.org - a volunteer-built GitHub Pages site used as a reference. The exported ZIP uses a union header banner instead of a local logo in the navigation bar.

sources.title: Sources & references
sources.intro: External resources cited in this guide. Always defer to your local executive and union servicing rep for official direction.

materials.title: Workshop Materials
materials.subtitle: Everything you need for the Aug 18 session and ongoing local communications

session.title: Aug 18 session - The Toolbox in 60 minutes
session.format: Pitch, live walkthrough, and hands-on - attendees leave with logo, board notice, social graphic, and website ZIP.

checklist.title: Attendee checklist
items: Import demo Brand Kit or complete onboarding
Export circular local logo (PNG)
Create and download a board notice (PDF)
Create one social graphic with Graphic Maker
Download website ZIP and read the included README

demoKit.title: Demo Brand Kit
demoKit.description: Pre-filled Local 243 brand settings for workshop attendees. Import on the Brand Kit page.

builtFrom.title: What this toolbox is built from
items: Website template - simplified from a volunteer local GitHub Pages site (reference: local243.org)
Brand colours - reference tenant defaults from official union graphics guidelines (customise in Brand Kit)
Blueprint chapters - volunteer comms best practices for local solidarity communications
Crisis guide - high-level guidance for local strike and bargaining communications
Accessibility - WCAG 2.1 AA and AODA commitments in the hub accessibility statement

allSources.title: Full source bibliography
allSources.intro: All external references used across guides, tools, and exported materials.

onboarding.title: Set up your local brand
onboarding.step3Description: Pick how your local appears in the header and on graphics. The header updates as you choose.

localLinks.title: Website & key links
localLinks.description: Optional URLs used across QR cards, posters, and the website template. Add socials or campaign links anytime.
localLinks.optionalHint: Optional - add your website and socials so posters and QR cards can reuse them.

brandKit.title: Brand Kit Manager
brandKit.description: Manage, export, and import your local branding - colours, logo, and key links ready for solidarity graphics

unionPreset.title: Union preset
unionPreset.description: Start from a common Canadian union colour palette and starter logo. OPSEU uses the bundled official lockup and mark; other unions use original starter wordmarks you can replace with your official assets.
unionPreset.label: Union preset
unionPreset.logoNote: OPSEU logos are the official pack already in this app. Other presets use starter wordmarks — not official trademarks. Upload your local’s real logo below when publishing.

logo.title: Logo
logo.description: Choose an OPSEU logo, upload your local logo, or use a short text mark.
logo.uploadHint: PNG, JPEG, WebP, or SVG up to 10 MB.
logo.useOfficialLogoHint: Official OPSEU/SEFPO lockup - recommended default when building your brand.
logo.noImageHint: Use a short text mark in the header instead of a logo image.
logo.logoTextHint: Up to 4 characters - e.g. LU or your local number.
logo.useLockupHint: Full OPSEU/SEFPO lockup - recommended default when building your brand.
logo.useMarkHint: Bars and trillium mark - compact for headers and circular logos.
logo.useSlitBlueHint: Bars and trillium with OPSEU/SEFPO cut through - for light backgrounds.
logo.useSlitWhiteHint: Same slit mark in white - for dark or brand-coloured backgrounds.

assets.title: Brand Asset Pack
assets.description: Colours, logos, and usage guidelines for local solidarity communications - customise everything in your Brand Kit so it fits your union.

guidelines.clearSpace: Always maintain clear space around the logo equal to the height of the wordmark
guidelines.noDistort: Do not stretch, rotate, or recolour the official logo
guidelines.primary: Lead with your primary brand colour on light backgrounds
guidelines.secondary: Use the graphics accent for text and highlights on brand-coloured or dark backgrounds
guidelines.contrast: Use a light or white logo treatment on dark backgrounds
guidelines.localMark: Local numbers and sub-text may be added below the logo using approved fonts
guidelines.questions: For questions about brand usage, contact your local or division communications chair

graphicMaker.title: News & Event Graphic Maker
graphicMaker.subtitle: Layouts that match Social Examples - solidarity, notices, results, spotlights, and thank-yous.
graphicMaker.headline: Headline

quoteCard.title: Quote Card Generator
quoteCard.subtitle: Short leadership quotes with local branding - ready for Instagram.
quoteCard.author: Attribution

flyerMaker.title: Picket / Rally Flyer Maker
flyerMaker.subtitle: High-contrast templates for social media and printing.

altTextAssistant.title: Alt-Text Assistant
altTextAssistant.subtitle: Draft image descriptions for social posts, then paste them into each platform’s alt-text field. This tool does not analyze images - you write the description.
altTextAssistant.starterLabel: Start from a common graphic type
altTextAssistant.altLabel: Alt text (for the image accessibility field)
altTextAssistant.altPlaceholder: Describe who or what is shown, the setting, and any text on the graphic…
altTextAssistant.captionLabel: Post caption (optional, for your own reference)
altTextAssistant.captionPlaceholder: Paste the caption you will post separately…
altTextAssistant.captionHint: Alt text and caption are different fields on most platforms. Do not put the full caption in the alt field.
altTextAssistant.draftLooksGood: Draft looks solid - no common issues detected. Still review the checklist before posting.

how.step1: Export or screenshot your graphic from Graphic Maker, Quote Card, Flyer, or another tool.
how.step2: Write alt text here that describes the visual content (not the caption).
how.step3: Copy the alt text and paste it into Instagram, Facebook, X, or LinkedIn’s “Edit alt text” / accessibility field when you post.
how.step4: Keep your usual caption in the normal post field.

checklist.visual: Describes what is visually in the image (people, signs, setting)
checklist.onImageText: Includes important text that appears in the graphic
checklist.localIdentity: Mentions the local number or union name if shown
checklist.concise: Stays concise - usually 1–2 sentences is enough
checklist.noImageOf: Does not start with “Image of” or “Photo of”

examples.good: “Blue Local 243 graphic: membership meeting Thursday 5pm, Room 204. Union logo top left; date and location in white text.”

meetingNotice.label: Meeting notice
meetingNotice.draft: Graphic announcing a membership meeting: [date], [time], [location]. Local [number] logo and key details in large text.

picketRally.label: Picket / rally
picketRally.draft: Solidarity graphic for a picket or rally: [date], [time], [location]. Bold headline “[main message]” with Local [number] branding.

quoteCard.label: Quote card
quoteCard.draft: Quote card with Local [number] branding. Quote: “[quote text]”. Attribution: [name / role].

memberSpotlight.label: Member spotlight
memberSpotlight.draft: Member spotlight graphic featuring [name / role]. Photo of [brief visual description]. Text: “[headline or title].” Local [number] branding.

textHeavyGraphic.label: Text-heavy graphic
textHeavyGraphic.draft: Informational Local [number] graphic. On-image text: “[transcribe the main lines].” Layout: [colours / logo placement].

issues.startsWithImageOf: Avoid starting with “Image of” or “Photo of” - screen readers already announce it as an image.
issues.tooShort: This looks too short. Add who/what is shown and any important on-image text.
issues.sameAsCaption: Alt text matches the caption. Describe the image itself; keep the caption in the post field.
issues.placeholderLeft: Replace the starter placeholders (brackets) with real details from your graphic.

consent.title: Member photo consent
consent.description: Before using a member's photo in social media graphics, confirm:
consent.item1: You have written or verbal consent from the member
consent.item2: The member understands the photo will be posted publicly
consent.item3: The photo does not contain confidential workplace information
consent.confirm: I confirm consent has been obtained

contrast.fail: Fails WCAG AA contrast - adjust colours for accessibility

crisisGuide.title: Strike & Crisis Comms Guide
crisisGuide.subtitle: High-level guidance for local unions during strikes, bargaining, and workplace crises
crisisGuide.intro: Central unions often provide sector-level bargaining support, but locals run their own member communications - especially on social media. This guide helps volunteer comms leads stay coordinated, lawful, and member-centred when stakes are high. Always defer to your local executive, union servicing rep, and any active strike or bargaining protocols.

strike.title: Strike action
items: Confirm all public messaging with your local president and union servicing rep before posting
Designate one or two spokespersons - members and media should hear one consistent local voice
Post daily picket schedules, gate locations, and safety reminders using branded graphics from this toolbox
Share solidarity and member stories with consent - never post photos without permission
Use your Brand Kit colours; follow division or sector campaign materials when coordinating beyond the local
Do not share negotiation details, member home addresses, or internal strategy on public channels
Direct questions about pay, duties, and return-to-work to the local strike committee - not comment threads

bargaining.title: Bargaining and countdown
items: Build momentum with factual updates - job security, workload, wages, and member priorities
Use consistent hashtags and graphics so members recognize official local posts
Encourage members to wear union colours and attend information meetings - post times and locations early
Share only what the bargaining team has approved for public release
Remind members to avoid debating management claims in public comment sections
Link to official union bargaining pages when available rather than paraphrasing from memory

layoffs.title: Layoffs and restructuring
items: Acknowledge member anxiety - be empathetic, factual, and calm
Direct affected members to stewards, union servicing reps, and EAP resources
Do not speculate about outcomes - share only confirmed local and employer statements
Use quote cards from elected leaders to show a unified local voice
Document management communications offline for grievance and consultation records
Avoid naming individuals at risk unless they have asked to be identified publicly

management.title: Management pushback and misinformation
items: Respond with facts and collective agreement language - never personal attacks
If management posts misinformation, publish one brief correction and pin it if needed
Escalate harassment, threats, or surveillance concerns to your union servicing rep immediately
Do not engage in prolonged public arguments with management accounts
Keep sensitive member issues in steward channels, not public posts
Screenshot problematic employer posts for the local executive and servicing rep

escalation.title: When to escalate
items: Media inquiries - route to your designated spokesperson or union communications
Legal threats, injunctions, or employer letters about conduct - executive and servicing rep immediately
Member safety incidents on the line or in the workplace - follow local emergency protocols first
Leaked confidential documents or suspected bad-faith bargaining - do not post; alert leadership
Viral misinformation about your local - coordinate a single correction rather than multiple replies

tools.title: Toolbox resources for crisis comms
tools.description: Use these on-device tools to keep visuals consistent when your local is under pressure:
items: Brand Kit - local number, colours, and logo ready for fast graphics
Board Notice Maker - workplace bulletin updates during strikes
Flyer Maker and Quote Card - leadership statements and picket updates
Graphic Maker - member spotlights with consent flow
Website Template - one stable link for members and media
Captions library - pre-written solidarity and bargaining posts

share.error: Couldn’t share. Copy the link from your address bar.

manifesto.title: Why UnionOps is free (and always will be)
manifesto.p1: Most software follows the same tired pattern: give you something useful, lock you in, then hit you with a monthly fee.
manifesto.p2: We hate that. Union dues should go to fighting for workers, not software subscriptions just to do basic organizing.
manifesto.promiseLead: UnionOps was built out of solidarity, not for profit.
manifesto.noSubsBody: This tool is free. No premium tiers, no paywalls, no hidden fees.
manifesto.noDataBody: We don't harvest your data or sell ads. Comms tools are local-first: graphics and brand kit stay in your browser. If you (or your local) host an Officer Hub, that instance holds hub records — and the operator of that host is the data controller. Prefer Canadian hosting for confidential work. No venture landlords, no surveillance business model.
manifesto.closing: UnionOps is built by union volunteers who wanted a solid tool for people doing the hard work on the ground.

footer.madeBy: UnionOps. Built in solidarity for union locals by union volunteers.
footer.privacy: Comms on-device; you control your hub

commitment.title: Our commitment
commitment.body: UnionOps is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for all members, including those with disabilities. We aim to conform to the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

features.title: Accessibility features
features.semanticHtml: Semantic HTML with proper heading hierarchy
features.focusIndicators: Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
features.contrast: Colour contrast checked against WCAG AA standards
features.reducedMotion: Reduced motion support via system settings and user preference
features.displaySettings: Adjustable font size, high contrast, and reduced motion settings
features.skipLink: Skip link to jump directly to main content
features.altTextTool: Alt-Text Assistant to draft image descriptions for social posts

limitations.title: Known limitations
limitations.body: Canvas-based image preview areas may not be fully accessible to screen reader users and may not reflect high-contrast display settings. All inputs that control the preview are accessible via standard form controls. Exported PNG/PDF files do not embed alt text - add descriptions in each platform’s alt-text field when posting (the Alt-Text Assistant helps you draft them).

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.body: If you encounter accessibility barriers while using this hub, please contact your local communications chair. We welcome feedback and are committed to continuous improvement.

display.title: Display settings
display.intro: Customize how the site looks and moves. Your choices are saved on this device.
display.fontSizeDescription: Choose a comfortable reading size. All text scales proportionally.
display.highContrastDescription: Stronger text and border contrast for easier reading.
display.reducedMotionDescription: Minimize animations and transitions, regardless of system settings.
display.previewText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Your local union deserves tools that work for everyone.

hub.title: Officer Hub
hub.loginSubtitle: Sign in to access grievance tracking and local tools
hub.demoHint: Demo: president@local243.ca / demo123 or solo@example.ca / demo123
hub.mfaSubtitle: Multi-factor authentication is required for confidential modules
hub.mfaError: Invalid code - enter any 6 digits in dev mode
hub.hybridCardDesc: Encrypt grievance and bumping backups, or keep a local encrypted slice on this device.
hub.qolCardDesc: Overdue dashboard, CA snippets, within-union templates, and officer handoff.

modules.commsDesc: Public social media tools and guides

hybrid.title: Hybrid data mode
hybrid.description: Encrypt grievance and bumping data for offline backup or a local-only slice. Your passphrase never leaves this browser.
hybrid.passphraseHint: Use a strong passphrase (8+ characters). You will need it to decrypt exports and restore a local slice.
hybrid.exportDesc: Download all confidential data you can access for this local as an encrypted file.
hybrid.importDesc: Decrypt a backup file and load it into the hub. Officers only.
hybrid.modeMerge: Merge - update matching IDs, keep other local records
hybrid.modeReplace: Replace - remove this local’s records, then import
hybrid.importSuccess: Imported {grievances} grievance(s) and {bumping} bumping case(s) ({removed} removed).
hybrid.localSliceDesc: Keep an encrypted copy in this browser for paranoid / offline locals. Comms tools stay on the central hub.
hybrid.localSaveSuccess: Encrypted slice saved in this browser.
hybrid.localCleared: Local slice cleared. Data mode set to central.
hybrid.dataModeDesc: Marks whether this device prefers central hub storage or a local encrypted slice reminder.
hybrid.dataModeHonest: Note: hub grievance/bumping screens still use the central store. Use Save/Restore below to sync an encrypted browser backup - local mode does not switch the live data path yet.
hybrid.modeLocalOn: Marked preference for local encrypted backup on this device.
hybrid.modeCentralOn: Prefer central hub storage on this device.

errors.passphraseShort: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters.
errors.invalidSlice: Server returned an invalid data slice.
errors.invalidFile: Not a valid hybrid encrypted file.
errors.noLocalSlice: No local slice found in this browser.
errors.needLocalSlice: Save a local slice before switching to local mode.

overdue.title: Overdue dashboard
overdue.subtitle: Prioritize late and upcoming grievance deadlines for your local.

snippets.title: CA clause snippets
snippets.subtitle: Union-scoped clause library for notes and drafts. Copy only - never auto-insert into member emails.

marketplace.title: Union template marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Share reusable templates within your union only - never across unions.
marketplace.scopeNote: Within-union sharing only. Cross-union access is blocked.
marketplace.publishError: Could not publish or remove template.

handoff.title: Officer handoff wizard
handoff.subtitle: Reassign open grievances and generate a handoff package for the incoming steward.
handoff.forbidden: Only local presidents and admins can run a handoff.
handoff.checklistHint: Confirm these steps before completing (advisory - not blocking).
handoff.doneDesc: Reassigned {count} grievance(s) to {name}. Download the package for your records.

comms.title: Member communication log
comms.hint: Record contacts with the member. Separate from internal officer notes. Never auto-send.

meetings.title: Meeting scheduler
meetings.hint: Schedule a meeting and download an ICS calendar invite.

mobile.toggleHint: Hides create/edit actions for a compact phone-friendly view. Turn off to edit assigned cases.
mobile.readOnlyBanner: Read-only mode is on. Viewing assigned cases only - turn off mobile steward mode to edit.

grievance.title: Grievance Tracking
grievance.subtitle: Track cases, deadlines, and officer notes
grievance.empty: No grievances yet. Create one to get started.
grievance.emailDraftWarning: Drafts only - review and send manually. Never auto-send.
grievance.memberPseudonymHint: Optional - use a pseudonym instead of real name

bumping.title: College Bumping
bumping.subtitle: Stability committee position comparison and decision log
bumping.disclaimer: Checklists assist committee process - not legal advice. Follow your collective agreement.
bumping.pdfError: Could not read PDF - paste text manually
bumping.compareHint: Differences highlighted - duties, qualifications, seniority markers
bumping.decisionHint: Record outcome for local records - committee decides, not software
